sections.paragraph:
διαπειράζω (s. πειράζω) (3 Macc 5:40; Jos., Ant. 2, 97; 15, 97) and διαπειράω (act. Plut., Pomp. 646 [51, 2]; POxy 1101, 21 [IV a.d.]; mostly mid. since Hdt.; Antiphon, Or. 5:34) 1 aor. inf. διαπειράσαι (Tdf.) or διαπειρᾶσαι (deStrycker) to find out by investigation, determine δ. εἰ ἵσταται whether (the child) could stand up GJs 6:1.

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

act. act. = active

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

aor. aor. = aorist

inf. inf. = infinitive

Tdf. Tdf. = CvTischendorf—List 1, beg.

deStrycker deStrycker = ÉdeStrycker; s. GJs—List 1

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 235
